questions.question: What should you do BEFORE attempting a rung on your Fear Ladder?

options.text: Jump straight into the activity without preparation to avoid overthinking
Practice Paced Breathing for 2-5 minutes and set a balanced, realistic thought
Wait until you feel no anxiety at all before starting
Tell yourself the activity will be easy and painless

questions.explanation: Before climbing a rung, you should (1) plan your specific step, (2) calm your body with 2-5 minutes of Paced Breathing to lower your baseline, and (3) set a balanced thought -- not 'This will be easy,' but something realistic like 'This will be uncomfortable, but I am safe and I can handle it.'

questions.question: What is the actual goal DURING an exposure exercise?

options.text: To feel zero anxiety throughout the exercise
To stay in the situation for the planned time so your brain learns nothing catastrophic happens
To distract yourself completely from the anxiety
To trigger the maximum amount of anxiety possible

questions.explanation: The goal is NOT to feel zero anxiety -- that is an unrealistic expectation. The goal is to STAY in the situation for the planned amount of time so your brain can learn that nothing catastrophic happened. The discomfort is actually the sign that the exercise is working.

questions.question: If your anxiety hits 10 out of 10 during an exposure exercise, what should you do?

options.text: Push through it no matter what to prove you can handle it
Stop, use the TIPP skill to calm down, and plan an easier step next time
Give up on the Fear Ladder entirely since it is not working
Switch immediately to a higher rung to build more tolerance

questions.explanation: If anxiety becomes a 10/10 crisis, that simply means the rung was too high. There is no failure, only data. Stop, use the TIPP skill to calm down, and plan to try an easier step next time. The goal is to stay in the 'productively uncomfortable' zone (4-7/10), not a traumatizing one.

questions.question: Why is self-celebration AFTER completing a ladder step so important?

options.text: It helps you forget about the anxiety you felt
It rewires your brain's reward system, linking the difficult activity with a positive outcome
It impresses other people with your progress
It releases endorphins that cancel out cortisol

questions.explanation: Celebrating your courage immediately after completing a step is what rewires your brain's reward system. Instead of the 'reward' being avoidance and relief, the new reward becomes the pride and accomplishment of having faced the fear. This creates a positive association with approaching rather than avoiding.

questions.question: Which skill should you use DURING an exposure exercise if your mind starts spiraling with anxious thoughts?

options.text: TIPP Skill
5-4-3-2-1 Grounding
Paired Muscle Relaxation only
Leave the situation immediately

questions.explanation: If your mind starts to spiral during exposure, the 5-4-3-2-1 Grounding skill is the best choice. It pulls your attention out of anxious thoughts and back into the present moment by engaging your senses -- noticing what you can see, feel, and hear right now.

title: Climbing the Ladder with Your Toolkit - Quiz
